steps.title: Use the shift property
steps.content: Data set B is data set A with $11$ subtracted from every $y$-value. Subtracting a constant from every $y$ shifts the line of best fit straight down by that constant: slope unchanged, $y$-intercept reduced by $11$.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept of A and subtract 11
steps.content: Extending the drawn line of best fit back to $x=0$ on the scatterplot gives roughly $y \approx 53.75$. So the line of best fit for set B has $y$-intercept $$53.75 - 11 = 42.75.$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
